betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Electric Arc Furnace§f

§aLarge automated multiblock furnace — smelt, cast, and produce silicon for advanced circuits!§f

The §bElectric Arc Furnace§f is a large automated multiblock machine that fills the role of both crucible and automated smelter. It is required for progression as the only machine that can produce §bSilicon§f from various sources (sand, flint, fiberglass, asbestos). Many mid-to-late game setups use an automated arc furnace for continuous ore-to-bar processing via its built-in fluid casting mode.

§6Crafting§f (Assembly Factory):
§b• §e12×§f Concrete
§b• §e8×§f Bakelite Bar
§b• §e16×§f Firebrick
§b• §e8×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• §e1×§f 10k-20Hz Transformer
§b• §e1×§f Analog Circuit Board

§6Operation§f:
§b• Power§f: §e20 kHE/s§f, §e2.5 MHE§f max buffer.
§b• Electrodes§f: Three required for operation. Four types with increasing durability:
  §b–§f Graphite (§e10§f operations)
  §b–§f Lanthanum (§e100§f operations)
  §b–§f Desh (§e500§f operations)
  §b–§f Saturnite (§e1,500§f operations)

§6Output Modes§f:
§b• Solid Mode§f: Simple ingot output — easiest to set up.
§b• Liquid Mode§f: Produces castable molten ores like a crucible, with a small output bonus. Place a Strand Caster under the outlet for rapid casting.

§6Key Recipes§f:
§b• Silicon§f: From Sand (1 nugget), Nether Quartz (3 nuggets), Asbestos (4 nuggets + quanta), Flint (4 nuggets + quanta), Fiberglass (4 nuggets + quanta).

§eTip§f: The Electric Arc Furnace is the primary source of §bSilicon§f, which is essential for higher-tier circuit boards. The §bLiquid Mode§f gives a small but free yield bonus — always use it once you have a Strand Caster set up. Start with Graphite Electrodes and upgrade to Desh for long production runs!
